desc: Identical AI technology produces opposite learning outcomes depending on whether it is guardrailed: practice-period gain versus unassisted-exam change across three experimental arms.
subtitle: Practice change vs. unassisted-exam change, signed against zero

figureTitle: Two rulers for the same effect size
desc: The same d=0.40 reads as impressive or exceptional depending on whether it sits against Hattie's inflated synthesis distribution or Kraft's realistic field-experiment distribution.
subtitle: One fixed d=0.40 marker on two distributions

rows.right: d=0.40 lands far in the high tail

figureTitle: Four AI-feasibility classes across the list
desc: The 252 influences distribute unevenly across four AI-feasibility classes, and the high-impact subset is dominated by AUGMENTED rather than NATIVE — augmentation territory, not replacement territory.
subtitle: All 252 influences vs. the above-hinge subset (122)

desc: A single influence row carries seven columns of three epistemic kinds — measured, classified, and hypothesized — and the hypothesized cell must be visually unmistakable from the measured one.
subtitle: Seven cells, three epistemic kinds

desc: The 130 below-hinge influences are three structurally different kinds — modest-but-real, negligible, and harmful — distinguished by effect-size band and by whether each entry is a choice or a condition.
subtitle: 130 below-hinge influences split by effect-size band

layers.label: Harmful band
layers.note: 17 influences, d -0.01 to -0.90; conditions vs. policy choices

desc: Every below-hinge number arrived by one of four distinct routes, each demanding an opposite response, so a single influence must be sorted by which route applies before any judgment.
subtitle: One entry node fans to four route-to-response pairs

desc: Worked examples should fade across three stages — full solution, completion problem, unsupported problem — with the expertise-reversal boundary marking where scaffolding must be retired.
subtitle: Scaffolding shrinks; expertise-reversal divides stages 2 and 3

data.note: Scaffolding region shrinks across the sequence

desc: A mobile-phone-ban's average effect conceals strong heterogeneity — concentrated in low achievers, near-zero for high achievers — which the single mid-pack number hides entirely.
subtitle: Beland & Murphy (2016), exam-gain effect size

desc: Spaced practice produces lower immediate recall but dramatically higher delayed recall than massed practice — the two retention curves cross, and the strain is the mechanism.
subtitle: Recall accuracy over days after study

figureTitle: Division of labor: logistics vs. retrieval
desc: AI runs the separable logistics around a practice routine — scheduling, varying, recording, flagging — while the learner's retrieval moment and the teacher's selection judgment stay structurally protected.
subtitle: AI arrows stop at the boundary; teacher judgment crosses it

desc: The tool's value appears only when planning-period time is accounted for — hours shift away from calendar-building and item-writing toward student-work analysis and feedback.
subtitle: Planning-period time before vs. after an automated review tool

desc: When the student builds structure first and AI critiques it, transfer appears on a cold assessment; when AI generates structure and the student fills it in, transfer does not — the fork is who performs the transforming act.
subtitle: Two paths from one transforming decision

stages.label: Student builds structure / AI generates structure
AI evaluates, student revises / student fills in
Transfer appears vs. transfer blocked on cold assessment

data.note: Path A builds transfer; Path B blocks it

desc: Metacognition is a closed control cycle — set goal, attempt, monitor, adjust, evaluate — and the learner must run every segment, or an external regulator runs the loop while the learner's capacity atrophies.
subtitle: The learner powers every segment

figureTitle: The fading schedule across four essays
desc: Scaffold intensity must decline across successive tasks — full scaffold, reduced, student-self-critique-first, AI-as-check-only — so the prompts thin out as the learner demonstrates unprompted self-monitoring.
subtitle: AI scaffold contracts as learner-led work expands

markers.label: AI used only to check the student's critique

desc: The same d=1.33 warrants opposite readings — a correlational description of a student asset (judgment accuracy) versus a vendor's causal claim of a purchasable payoff.
subtitle: One d=1.33 value, two structurally different readings

rows.left: Judgment accuracy as a pre-existing asset

desc: A self-regulated learner runs a loop — predict, plan, attempt, monitor, evaluate, adjust — where each cycle improves the work and sharpens the model of self, and the prediction-error is the loop's most valuable output.
subtitle: Prediction-error is the curriculum

desc: At each loop segment AI has a legitimate logistics-and-feedback role and a forbidden first-person substitution, and the gate runs cleanly between them.
subtitle: Safe AI role (left) vs. forbidden substitution (right), per segment

rows.right: Make the prediction for the learner

figureType: systems diagram (two-pipeline flow with single handoff)
desc: AI completes the detection pipeline entirely (context variables, model, risk score, flag) but touches nothing in the response pipeline; the two systems connect only at human review.
subtitle: Single handoff: flag to human review

figureTitle: Working-memory load reduction in a task
desc: The group's one genuine lever is task redesign — the same multi-step procedure made to fit a narrower working-memory channel by externalizing steps and adding an adjacent worked example.
subtitle: Before: four steps held in mind. After: steps externalized

rows.left: Four steps held simultaneously in mind
rows.right: Steps externalized as an ordered list
Adjacent worked example beside the problem

desc: Authentic relational investment compounds slowly into high trust, while a simulated relationship's metrics rise then collapse below the starting baseline in a single discovery event.
subtitle: Slow compounding vs. instantaneous collapse below baseline

figureTitle: AI on the perimeter, human at the event
figureType: systems diagram (before/after perimeter around a protected core)
desc: AI's legitimate role sits before and after the human relational event — translating, freeing time, detecting gaps, preparing, documenting — but never constitutes the event itself.
subtitle: AI arrows stop at the boundary; only human presence crosses

figureTitle: Two halves, two owners, two learners
figureType: structural schematic (split panel with two learner rows)
desc: Instructional clarity splits into an AI-delegable planned half and a human-only live half, and the same delegation builds the live half for a novice through struggle but is mere time-saving for an expert.
subtitle: Planned half (delegable) vs. live half (human-only)

rows.left: Novice: planned-half work builds the live half
Expert: planned half is detached time-cost
rows.right: Live half already built; pure time arbitrage

figureType: cycle (real-time feedback loop with emphasized detection node)
desc: Expert clarity is a real-time loop — explain, check, detect, adjust, re-explain — whose detection step is the heart and which a script-bound teacher structurally cannot run.
subtitle: Detect is the heart; a script reaches explain but is blocked from detect

figureTitle: Draft-then-correct workflow and the delta log
desc: Expert free recall yields an incomplete curriculum, but an AI draft corrected by the expert converts extraction into editing — and the corrections themselves (the delta log) are the curriculum's most valuable column.
subtitle: Path A free recall vs. Path B AI draft then expert correction

stages.label: Path B: AI draft then expert correction
Validated curriculum + delta log (most valuable output)

data.note: Corrections surface tacit knowledge only against a concrete wrong thing

figureType: conceptual map (central node with four repair spokes)
desc: All four influences repair part of one double-blindness — an accurate model of the task and of the learner's current knowledge — with each row fixing a different component.
subtitle: Four repair spokes into one shared model

nodes.label: Accurate model of task and learner

figureTitle: The Bastani curve in a help channel
desc: An executive-help AI channel lifts the supervised metric (practice performance) while the unaided metric (proctored certification) flattens then drops — the same assisted/unassisted divergence in domain-specific form.
subtitle: Practice performance vs. unaided certification over weeks

data.axisLabel: Score over weeks of AI-channel use
